trigger_evals.query: make a conventional commit for the staged files
split these changes into two commits
open a pull request for my branch
show the git diff without committing anything
draft a conventional commit message for my current diff
improve the title and description for my pull request

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Commits a single obvious README change without conversational approval using a subject-only Conventional Commit message.
checks: Does NOT ask the user for approval before committing
Commit subject starts with 'fix:'
Commit message has NO body paragraph
Only README.md is included in the commit

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Commits the intentionally staged files immediately with a Conventional Commit message while preserving and reporting other unstaged work.
checks: Commits only src/auth.py and src/auth_test.py
Unstaged files (config.yaml, docs/setup.md, src/utils.py) are NOT staged or committed
Mentions the unstaged files as an FYI to the user
Created commit message uses a conventional commit prefix
Does NOT ask the user to approve the files or commit message

behavior_evals.prompt: split these changes into two commits
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Creates separate commits without a review pause when unrelated changes map cleanly to separate whole files.
checks: Creates at least 2 separate commits for the unrelated changes
Does NOT ask the user to approve the groups or commit messages
Each created commit has an appropriate conventional commit prefix
Does not bundle all changes into a single commit and leaves the working tree clean

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Stops immediately when the repo has unmerged paths or an in-progress sequencer operation instead of attempting a commit.
checks: Detects unmerged/conflicted paths or an in-progress merge, rebase, cherry-pick, or revert
Does NOT stage any files
Does NOT create a commit
Tells the user what must be resolved first

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Checks whether the current non-default branch matches the primary change and stops when the branch clearly points at unrelated work.
checks: Evaluates whether the current branch name is related to the primary change being committed
Does NOT assume any non-default branch is acceptable
Warns when the branch clearly does not match the change
Asks whether to create or switch to a more appropriate branch before committing

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: When branch relevance is ambiguous, surfaces the ambiguity and asks the user instead of guessing.
checks: Evaluates branch relevance before committing
Treats an ambiguous branch-to-change mapping as a stop condition
Asks the user whether to keep using the current branch or move the commit elsewhere
Does NOT create a commit before the ambiguity is resolved

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Stops on detached HEAD and warns the user before any staging or commit.
checks: Detects detached HEAD from repo state
Warns the user that committing on detached HEAD is unsafe
Does NOT stage any files
Does NOT create a commit

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: When a file is partially staged, commits only the staged hunks and leaves unstaged hunks untouched.
checks: Commits only the staged hunks from src/auth.py
Does NOT restage src/auth.py
Mentions that unstaged hunks remain in the working tree
Commit message uses a conventional commit prefix

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Does not auto-split unrelated unstaged hunks inside the same file; instead it tells the user to pre-stage or accept a single commit.
checks: Does NOT propose separate commits for unrelated unstaged hunks in the same file
Does NOT use interactive staging such as git add -p
Tells the user to pre-stage the intended hunks or accept a single commit
Does NOT create a commit before the user resolves the ambiguity

behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Gathers read-only git context with minimal tool chatter, checks sequencer sentinel paths in the same inspection pass, and keeps mutating steps separate.
checks: Uses git status --short --branch for branch and worktree state
Gathers read-only status, diffs, and recent log in a single Bash invocation when possible
Checks git-dir sentinel paths for merge, cherry-pick, revert, and rebase state before mutating
Uses git symbolic-ref --quiet --short HEAD when branch identity is needed
Keeps staging, committing, and verification as separate mutating steps

behavior_evals.prompt: Draft the commit message for these changes, but do not stage or commit anything.
behavior_evals.expected_behavior: Produces a reviewable Conventional Commit message whose formatting communicates the primary change and motivation without mutating the repository.
checks: Does NOT stage files or create a commit
Presents the complete message in a text code block
Uses a valid Conventional Commit type and keeps the subject under 72 characters with no trailing period
Separates the subject and body with a blank line
Uses a concise prose body that explains motivation or behavioral impact rather than listing files
Does not invent an issue identifier or breaking-change footer
